title: Spot the compounder
intro: Company X looks dull and a little expensive. Open the clues, then diagnose what you're really looking at.

facts.label: Return on capital
facts.value: Dipped 3 years ago during a big distribution build-out; has risen for 6 straight quarters since.

facts.label: Earnings
facts.value: Growing ~24% a year for 4 years; the share price has roughly tracked earnings, not run ahead of them.

facts.label: Debt
facts.value: Low and falling; interest easily covered by operating profit.

facts.label: Capital allocation
facts.value: ~70% of profit reinvested into the core business; no unrelated acquisitions.

facts.label: Market
facts.value: Addressable market is large and still fragmented/unorganised — plenty of runway.

question: Best read on Company X?
choices: A hype stock — price running on a story, not fundamentals
An emerging wealth creator — moat forming, earnings compounding, disciplined reinvestment
A value trap — cheap because the business is quietly deteriorating
A mature incumbent at risk of complacency
debrief: The tell-tale pattern: ROCE dipped during a build phase then inflected upward (moat forming), earnings compound and the price tracks them (not a story), debt is low, profits are reinvested into the core, and there's a long runway. That's the anatomy of an emerging compounder — not hype, not a trap, and not yet a complacent incumbent.
